_comment: Tests Node 7b rescheduler routing correctness: reschedule_cancelled vs cancel_intent confusion, abandonment silence, booking success wrap_up contract, and RESCHEDULE BYPASS path.

scenarios.name: Signal 0.5 — cancel intent mid-rebook uses reschedule_cancelled not cancel_intent

history.content: So we're booking you in for another Physiotherapy appointment — is that right?
Actually I want to cancel that other one too.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent calls universal_router intent='reschedule_cancelled' with payload {reschedule_mode: '', cancellation_completed: 'none'} and zero spoken output. FAIL if: agent calls intent='cancel_intent' (wrong intent from this node), or speaks any words before routing.

scenarios.name: ABANDONMENT — caller declines rebook, agent must route silently not farewell aloud

history.content: So we're booking you in for another Massage appointment — is that right?
No thanks, I'm all good.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent calls universal_router intent='wrap_up' with zero spoken output. FAIL if: agent says 'Goodbye' or any farewell phrase before calling the tool, or fails to call the tool at all.

scenarios.name: BOOKING STEP success — verbatim message + wrap_up in same turn

history.content: Let me lock that in.
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"success": true, "message": "You're booked in for Physiotherapy on Thursday at 10am with Sarah."}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent speaks 'You're booked in for Physiotherapy on Thursday at 10am with Sarah.' verbatim AND calls universal_router intent='wrap_up' in the same turn with zero additional spoken output. FAIL if: agent appends questions, omits the wrap_up tool call, or paraphrases instead of speaking verbatim.

scenarios.name: RESCHEDULE BYPASS — reschedule_mode=true must skip confirmation question

history.content: [universal_router response]: {"uni_router_intent": "cancellation_done", "reschedule_mode": "true", "appointment_type": "Osteopathy", "appointment_type_id": "123456"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent skips all ENTRY confirmation steps and proceeds directly to AVAILABILITY STEP — asks 'What day and time works for you?' without first asking 'So we're booking you in for another Osteopathy — is that right?'. FAIL if: agent asks the confirmation question before asking for day/time.

scenarios.name: CATEGORY UNKNOWN — 'my appointment' is not a valid category name

history.content: What type of appointment were you looking to rebook?
The one I just cancelled.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent asks 'What type of appointment were you looking to rebook?' again (Signal 0 re-ask) with no tool call. FAIL if: agent accepts 'the one I just cancelled' as a valid category and proceeds to the confirmation question or availability step.

scenarios.name: POLICY WARNING — caller confirms, agent must speak then re-book with override

history.content: Let me lock that in.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"success": false, "error": "reschedule_cancellation_policy_warning", "message": "Just so you know, because your new appointment is more than 5 days from the original date, the clinic's 216-hour cancellation policy applies and a cancellation fee may apply. Would you still like to go ahead with the reschedule?"}

Just so you know, because your new appointment is more than 5 days from the original date, the clinic's 216-hour cancellation policy applies and a cancellation fee may apply. Would you still like to go ahead with the reschedule?
Yes, go ahead.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent says 'Let me lock that in.' AND calls smart_voice_agent intent='book' with confirm_policy_override=true in the same turn. FAIL if: agent omits the spoken phrase before calling the tool, omits confirm_policy_override=true, calls universal_router instead, or asks for a new date/time.

scenarios.name: TIME NOT AVAILABLE (branch C) — 12:05 not in slots, offer 12 PM and 12:15 PM

history.content: What day and time works for you?
July 21 at 12:05.

[smart_voice_agent availability response]: {"success": true, "found": true, "practitioners": [{"dates": [{"date": "2026-07-21", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["11 AM", "11:15 AM", "11:30 AM", "11:45 AM"], "afternoon": ["12 PM", "12:15 PM", "12:30 PM", "12:45 PM", "1 PM"], "evening": []}}]}], "agent_instruction": "SLOT INTEGRITY: Read appointment times from slot_groups exactly as they appear."}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent says '12:05' is not available on that day and offers '12 PM' and '12:15 PM' as the nearest alternatives, then waits. FAIL if: agent proceeds to book 12:05 (rogue time not in the slot list), or offers times that are not adjacent to 12:05 in the list.

scenarios.name: NO SLOTS FOR DATE (branch B) — FNA fallback, offer alternative days not times

history.content: What day and time works for you?
July 21 at 3 PM.

[smart_voice_agent availability response]: {"success": true, "found": true, "practitioners": [{"dates": [{"date": "2026-07-22", "day_of_week": "Wednesday, July 22nd", "slot_groups": {"morning": [], "afternoon": ["2 PM", "2:15 PM", "3 PM"], "evening": []}}, {"date": "2026-07-24", "day_of_week": "Friday, July 24th", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9 AM", "9:15 AM"], "afternoon": [], "evening": []}}], "slot_groups": {}}], "agent_instruction": "The requested date has no availability. Next available: Wednesday July 22nd and Friday July 24th. Ask which day works."}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent follows the agent_instruction and offers Wednesday July 22 and Friday July 24 as alternative days, then waits for the caller to choose a day. FAIL if: agent says '3 PM isn't available' and tries to offer adjacent times (branch C logic applied to an FNA response with no slots for the requested date).

scenarios.name: POLICY WARNING — caller declines, agent must ABANDON

history.content: Just so you know, because your new appointment is more than 5 days from the original date, the clinic's 216-hour cancellation policy applies and a cancellation fee may apply. Would you still like to go ahead with the reschedule?
No thanks, forget it.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent calls universal_router intent='wrap_up' with zero spoken output (ABANDON path). FAIL if: agent tries to find another slot, re-reads the policy, or speaks any farewell before calling the tool.

scenarios.name: RESCHEDULE BYPASS ENTRY — entry turn must ask time question only, no tool call

history.content: Hi, I'd like to reschedule my appointment with Jessica today.
Let's find your booked appointment first.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"success": true, "message": "Found appointment: Physiotherapy on Thursday June 25 at 11 AM with Jessica Rowe.", "appointment_id": "appt_12345", "appointment_type": "Physiotherapy", "appointment_type_id": "type_789", "practitioner_id": "prac_456", "business_id": "bus_123"}
I found your appointment on Thursday, June 25 at 11 AM with Jessica Rowe. Is that the one you'd like to cancel?
Correct, yes.
Perfect, let me find you a new time.

[universal_router response]: {"uni_router_intent": "wrap_reschedule", "reschedule_mode": "true", "cancellation_completed": "false", "appointment_type": "Physiotherapy", "appointment_type_id": "type_789", "practitioner_id": "prac_456", "appointment_id": "appt_12345", "business_id": "bus_123"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent speaks ONLY 'What day and time works for you?' and makes NO tool call (smart_voice_agent must NOT be called on this entry turn — no cancel, no availability, nothing). FAIL if: agent calls smart_voice_agent or universal_router with any intent before the caller has named a new time, even if agent also asks the time question in the same turn.

scenarios.name: RESCHEDULE BYPASS ENTRY — after caller names time, THEN call availability

history.content: Hi, I'd like to reschedule my appointment with Jessica today.
Let's find your booked appointment first.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"success": true, "message": "Found appointment: Physiotherapy on Thursday June 25 at 11 AM with Jessica Rowe.", "appointment_id": "appt_12345", "appointment_type": "Physiotherapy", "appointment_type_id": "type_789", "practitioner_id": "prac_456", "business_id": "bus_123"}
I found your appointment on Thursday, June 25 at 11 AM with Jessica Rowe. Is that the one you'd like to cancel?
Correct, yes.
Perfect, let me find you a new time.

[universal_router response]: {"uni_router_intent": "wrap_reschedule", "reschedule_mode": "true", "cancellation_completed": "false", "appointment_type": "Physiotherapy", "appointment_type_id": "type_789", "practitioner_id": "prac_456", "appointment_id": "appt_12345", "business_id": "bus_123"}
What day and time works for you?
Friday at 11 AM.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent calls smart_voice_agent intent='availability' with date=next Friday's date and appointment_time=11:00, appointment_type_id='type_789', practitioner_id='prac_456'. FAIL if: agent asks a confirmation question ('So we're booking you in for another Physiotherapy — is that right?') instead of calling availability, or calls universal_router instead of smart_voice_agent.

scenarios.name: ANCHOR DAY DENIED (branch A2) — caller's day has no morning but has afternoon; never claim it's unavailable

history.content: I don't have anything on Tuesday July seven. Beyond that, I have availability Wednesday July eighth, Thursday July ninth, and Saturday July eleventh. Would another day work?
Is there something Wednesday July eighth in the morning?

[smart_voice_agent availability response]: {"success": true, "found": true, "band_filtered_range": true, "multi_day": true, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_id": "120513", "practitioner_name": "Rebekah", "dates": [{"date": "2026-07-11", "day_of_week": "Saturday, July 11th", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["8:30 AM", "9:15 AM", "10 AM", "10:45 AM", "11:30 AM"]}}]}], "anchor_other_bands": {"date": "2026-07-08", "day_of_week": "Wednesday, July 8th", "practitioner_name": "Rebekah", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["2 PM", "2:45 PM", "3:30 PM"]}, "bands": ["afternoon"]}}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent offers BOTH Wednesday the 8th afternoon (starting at 2 / 2 PM) AND Saturday the 11th morning (8:30 and 11:30) in one turn, then waits. FAIL if: agent says Wednesday the 8th is unavailable / has nothing, OR offers only Saturday and drops Wednesday entirely, OR invents Wednesday morning times.

scenarios.name: STICKY BAND (Fix B) — caller stated 1 PM earlier; bare day later must carry preferred_time=afternoon

history.content: What day and time works for you?
Tuesday the 7th at 1 PM.

[smart_voice_agent availability response]: {"success": true, "found": true, "response_type": "summary", "available_dates": [{"date": "2026-07-09", "day_of_week": "Thursday, July 9th"}], "message": "I don't have anything on Tuesday July 7. Beyond that, I have Thursday July 9th."}
I don't have anything on Tuesday the 7th. Beyond that I have Thursday the 9th. Would another day work?
Yeah let's try Thursday.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent calls smart_voice_agent intent='availability' for Thursday 2026-07-09 with preferred_time='afternoon' carried forward from the caller's earlier '1 PM'. FAIL if: agent omits preferred_time, or sends preferred_time='morning' or any band other than afternoon.

scenarios.name: TARGET TIME KNOWN (new) — caller named new time in same breath as cancel confirm; timeframe_raw pre-populated on node entry

history.content: Yes, cancel it — and can we do next Saturday, the 18th, instead?

[universal_router response]: {"uni_router_intent": "wrap_reschedule", "reschedule_mode": "true", "cancellation_completed": "false", "appointment_type": "Physiotherapy", "appointment_type_id": "type_789", "practitioner_id": "prac_456", "appointment_id": "appt_12345", "business_id": "bus_123", "timeframe_raw": "next Saturday, the 18th"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent does NOT ask 'What day and time works for you?' — instead extracts the date from timeframe_raw ('next Saturday, the 18th') and calls smart_voice_agent intent='availability' (or find_next_available) for that date in this same turn, with appointment_type_id='type_789' and practitioner_id='prac_456'. FAIL if: agent asks the day/time question, ignores timeframe_raw, or fails to call any tool this turn.

scenarios.name: TARGET TIME UNKNOWN (new) — reschedule_mode true, appointment_type_id set, timeframe_raw empty — must still ask

history.content: Yes, go ahead and cancel it.

[universal_router response]: {"uni_router_intent": "wrap_reschedule", "reschedule_mode": "true", "cancellation_completed": "false", "appointment_type": "Physiotherapy", "appointment_type_id": "type_789", "practitioner_id": "prac_456", "appointment_id": "appt_12345", "business_id": "bus_123", "timeframe_raw": ""}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent asks 'What day and time works for you?' and makes NO tool call this turn (timeframe_raw is empty, so this is the ordinary bypass path, unchanged by the new TARGET TIME KNOWN branch). FAIL if: agent skips the question or invents a date/time with no source.

scenarios.name: KNOWN-UNAVAILABLE DAY (Fix D) — day already shown unavailable, answer from memory with no tool call

history.content: I don't have anything on Tuesday the 7th. Beyond that I have Wednesday the 8th, Thursday the 9th, and Saturday the 11th. Would another day work?
What about Friday the 10th?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent answers from memory that Friday the 10th has nothing either and re-offers the 8th, 9th, and 11th, making NO tool call (Friday the 10th is within the already-searched window and was not offered). FAIL if: agent calls smart_voice_agent for Friday the 10th, or claims Friday the 10th is available.
